# solution.py
"""MultiAgentTaskScheduler.

A self-contained, dependency-free engine for coordinating and executing tasks
across multiple agents.  It provides:

* Task definition (name, description, priority, required resources, estimate).
* Assignment of tasks to capable agents with automatic, dynamic (re)assignment
  driven by agent availability and task urgency (including preemption).
* Real-time progress feedback (completion %, estimated time to completion and
  reported issues).
* Inter-agent communication: direct/broadcast chat messages and a shared
  status board, plus scheduler-generated notifications.
* Adaptation to change: adding tasks, completing tasks early, or agents going
  offline automatically reorganises the queue and notifies affected agents.
* A query/monitoring layer: filter, sort and search tasks, inspect per-agent
  performance and render a textual dashboard.
* A full history log of every task lifecycle event (start/end times, assigned
  agents and notes/feedback).

The module is import-safe (no side effects on import) and thread-safe via a
re-entrant lock, so it can be embedded in a larger real-time application.  A
scripted demonstration runs under ``python solution.py``.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import itertools
import threading
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from enum import Enum, IntEnum
from typing import Callable, Dict, List, Optional, Sequence, Set, Tuple

__all__ = [
    "SchedulerError",
    "ValidationError",
    "NotFoundError",
    "Priority",
    "TaskStatus",
    "AgentStatus",
    "MessageType",
    "Message",
    "HistoryEntry",
    "Task",
    "Agent",
    "MultiAgentTaskScheduler",
]


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
# Exceptions
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
class SchedulerError(Exception):
    """Base class for all scheduler errors."""


class ValidationError(SchedulerError):
    """Raised when user supplied data fails validation."""


class NotFoundError(SchedulerError):
    """Raised when a referenced task or agent does not exist."""


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
# Enumerations
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
class Priority(IntEnum):
    """Task priority. Higher value == more urgent."""

    LOW = 1
    MEDIUM = 2
    HIGH = 3
    CRITICAL = 4

    @property
    def label(self) -> str:
        return self.name.capitalize()

    @classmethod
    def coerce(cls, value: "Priority | int | str") -> "Priority":
        """Best-effort conversion of *value* into a :class:`Priority`."""
        if isinstance(value, Priority):
            return value
        if isinstance(value, bool):  # bool is an int subclass - reject it.
            raise ValidationError(f"Invalid priority: {value!r}")
        if isinstance(value, int):
            try:
                return cls(value)
            except ValueError as exc:
                raise ValidationError(f"Invalid priority value: {value!r}") from exc
        if isinstance(value, str):
            key = value.strip().upper()
            if key in cls.__members__:
                return cls[key]
            if key.isdigit():
                return cls.coerce(int(key))
        raise ValidationError(f"Invalid priority: {value!r}")


class TaskStatus(Enum):
    PENDING = "pending"
    ASSIGNED = "assigned"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
    BLOCKED = "blocked"
    COMPLETED = "completed"
    FAILED = "failed"
    CANCELLED = "cancelled"

    @property
    def is_terminal(self) -> bool:
        return self in (TaskStatus.COMPLETED, TaskStatus.FAILED, TaskStatus.CANCELLED)

    @property
    def is_active(self) -> bool:
        """Active == occupies an agent slot."""
        return self in (
            TaskStatus.ASSIGNED,
            TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS,
            TaskStatus.BLOCKED,
        )


class AgentStatus(Enum):
    AVAILABLE = "available"
    OFFLINE = "offline"


class MessageType(Enum):
    CHAT = "chat"
    STATUS = "status"
    NOTIFICATION = "notification"


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
# Helpers
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
def _normalise_resources(resources) -> Set[str]:
    """Normalise an iterable of resource/capability names to a lowercase set."""
    if resources is None:
        return set()
    if isinstance(resources, str):
        resources = [resources]
    out: Set[str] = set()
    for item in resources:
        if item is None:
            continue
        token = str(item).strip().lower()
        if token:
            out.add(token)
    return out


def format_duration(seconds: Optional[float]) -> str:
    """Render *seconds* as a compact human readable string."""
    if seconds is None:
        return "unknown"
    seconds = max(0, int(round(seconds)))
    if seconds < 60:
        return f"{seconds}s"
    minutes, sec = divmod(seconds, 60)
    if minutes < 60:
        return f"{minutes}m {sec}s"
    hours, minutes = divmod(minutes, 60)
    return f"{hours}h {minutes}m"


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
# Data models
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
@dataclass
class Message:
    """A chat message, status update or scheduler notification."""

    id: str
    sender: str
    content: str
    timestamp: datetime
    recipient: Optional[str] = None  # None == broadcast
    type: MessageType = MessageType.CHAT

    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
        return {
            "id": self.id,
            "sender": self.sender,
            "recipient": self.recipient or "*",
            "type": self.type.value,
            "timestamp": self.timestamp.isoformat(),
            "content": self.content,
        }

    def __str__(self) -> str:
        ts = self.timestamp.strftime("%H:%M:%S")
        to = self.recipient or "ALL"
        return f"[{ts}] ({self.type.value}) {self.sender} -> {to}: {self.content}"


@dataclass
class HistoryEntry:
    """An immutable record of something that happened in the scheduler."""

    id: str
    timestamp: datetime
    event: str
    task_id: Optional[str] = None
    agent_id: Optional[str] = None
    details: str = ""

    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
        return {
            "id": self.id,
            "timestamp": self.timestamp.isoformat(),
            "event": self.event,
            "task_id": self.task_id,
            "agent_id": self.agent_id,
            "details": self.details,
        }

    def __str__(self) -> str:
        ts = self.timestamp.strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
        bits = [f"[{ts}]", self.event]
        if self.task_id:
            bits.append(f"task={self.task_id}")
        if self.agent_id:
            bits.append(f"agent={self.agent_id}")
        if self.details:
            bits.append(f"- {self.details}")
        return " ".join(bits)


@dataclass
class Task:
    """A unit of work to be executed by an agent."""

    id: str
    name: str
    description: str = ""
    priority: Priority = Priority.MEDIUM
    required_resources: Set[str] = field(default_factory=set)
    estimated_duration: Optional[float] = None  # seconds
    status: TaskStatus = TaskStatus.PENDING
    assigned_agent_id: Optional[str] = None
    progress: float = 0.0  # 0..100
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    assigned_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    started_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    completed_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    seq: int = 0  # creation order, for stable sorting
    notes: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    issues: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    feedback: Optional[str] = None

    # -- derived ---------------------------------------------------------- #
    def time_to_completion(self, now: datetime) -> Optional[float]:
        """Estimated seconds remaining, or ``None`` when unknown."""
        if self.status.is_terminal or self.progress >= 100:
            return 0.0
        remaining = 100.0 - self.progress
        if self.started_at is not None and self.progress > 0:
            elapsed = (now - self.started_at).total_seconds()
            if elapsed > 0:
                rate = self.progress / elapsed  # percent per second
                if rate > 0:
                    return remaining / rate
        if self.estimated_duration:
            return self.estimated_duration * remaining / 100.0
        return None

    def estimated_completion_time(self, now: datetime) -> Optional[datetime]:
        eta = self.time_to_completion(now)
        return None if eta is None else now + timedelta(seconds=eta)

    def to_dict(self, now: Optional[datetime] = None) -> dict:
        now = now or datetime.now()
        return {
            "id": self.id,
            "name": self.name,
            "description": self.description,
            "priority": self.priority.label,
            "required_resources": sorted(self.required_resources),
            "estimated_duration": self.estimated_duration,
            "status": self.status.value,
            "assigned_agent_id": self.assigned_agent_id,
            "progress": round(self.progress, 2),
            "eta_seconds": self.time_to_completion(now),
            "created_at": self.created_at.isoformat() if self.created_at else None,
            "started_at": self.started_at.isoformat() if self.started_at else None,
            "completed_at": self.completed_at.isoformat()
            if self.completed_at
            else None,
            "notes": list(self.notes),
            "issues": list(self.issues),
            "feedback": self.feedback,
        }


@dataclass
class Agent:
    """A worker that executes tasks."""

    id: str
    name: str
    capabilities: Set[str] = field(default_factory=set)
    status: AgentStatus = AgentStatus.AVAILABLE
    max_concurrent_tasks: int = 1
    active_task_ids: Set[str] = field(default_factory=set)
    completed_count: int = 0
    failed_count: int = 0
    total_completion_seconds: float = 0.0

    # -- derived ---------------------------------------------------------- #
    @property
    def load(self) -> int:
        return len(self.active_task_ids)

    @property
    def is_online(self) -> bool:
        return self.status == AgentStatus.AVAILABLE

    def is_available(self) -> bool:
        return self.is_online and self.load < self.max_concurrent_tasks

    def can_handle(self, task: "Task") -> bool:
        return task.required_resources.issubset(self.capabilities)

    @property
    def success_rate(self) -> float:
        total = self.completed_count + self.failed_count
        return 1.0 if total == 0 else self.completed_count / total

    @property
    def avg_completion_seconds(self) -> Optional[float]:
        if self.completed_count == 0:
            return None
        return self.total_completion_seconds / self.completed_count

    def display_status(self) -> str:
        if self.status == AgentStatus.OFFLINE:
            return "offline"
        if self.load >= self.max_concurrent_tasks:
            return "busy"
        if self.load > 0:
            return "working"
        return "idle"

    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
        return {
            "id": self.id,
            "name": self.name,
            "capabilities": sorted(self.capabilities),
            "status": self.display_status(),
            "online": self.is_online,
            "load": self.load,
            "max_concurrent_tasks": self.max_concurrent_tasks,
            "active_task_ids": sorted(self.active_task_ids),
            "completed": self.completed_count,
            "failed": self.failed_count,
            "success_rate": round(self.success_rate, 3),
            "avg_completion_seconds": self.avg_completion_seconds,
        }


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
# Scheduler
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
class MultiAgentTaskScheduler:
    """Coordinates the assignment and execution of tasks across agents."""

    SCHEDULER_ID = "SCHEDULER"

    def __init__(
        self,
        *,
        clock: Optional[Callable[[], datetime]] = None,
        enable_preemption: bool = True,
    ) -> None:
        self._clock: Callable[[], datetime] = clock or datetime.now
        self.enable_preemption = enable_preemption

        self._tasks: Dict[str, Task] = {}
        self._agents: Dict[str, Agent] = {}
        self._messages: List[Message] = []
        self._history: List[HistoryEntry] = []
        self._status_board: Dict[str, Tuple[str, datetime]] = {}

        self._task_seq = itertools.count(1)
        self._order_seq = itertools.count(1)
        self._agent_seq = itertools.count(1)
        self._msg_seq = itertools.count(1)
        self._hist_seq = itertools.count(1)

        self._lock = threading.RLock()

    # -- internal utilities ---------------------------------------------- #
    def _now(self) -> datetime:
        return self._clock()

    def _log(
        self,
        event: str,
        *,
        task_id: Optional[str] = None,
        agent_id: Optional[str] = None,
        details: str = "",
    ) -> HistoryEntry:
        entry = HistoryEntry(
            id=f"H-{next(self._hist_seq)}",
            timestamp=self._now(),
            event=event,
            task_id=task_id,
            agent_id=agent_id,
            details=details,
        )
        self._history.append(entry)
        return entry

    def _emit(
        self,
        sender: str,
        content: str,
        *,
        recipient: Optional[str] = None,
        type: MessageType = MessageType.CHAT,
    ) -> Message:
        msg = Message(
            id=f"M-{next(self._msg_seq)}",
            sender=sender,
            content=content,
            timestamp=self._now(),
            recipient=recipient,
            type=type,
        )
        self._messages.append(msg)
        return msg

    def _notify(self, agent_id: Optional[str], content: str) -> None:
        self._emit(
            self.SCHEDULER_ID,
            content,
            recipient=agent_id,
            type=MessageType.NOTIFICATION,
        )

    def _require_task(self, task_id: str) -> Task:
        try:
            return self._tasks[task_id]
        except KeyError:
            raise NotFoundError(f"Unknown task: {task_id!r}") from None

    def _require_agent(self, agent_id: str) -> Agent:
        try:
            return self._agents[agent_id]
        except KeyError:
            raise NotFoundError(f"Unknown agent: {agent_id!r}") from None

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Agent management
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    def register_agent(
        self,
        name: str,
        capabilities=None,
        *,
        max_concurrent_tasks: int = 1,
        agent_id: Optional[str] = None,
    ) -> Agent:
        """Register a new agent and (re)balance the queue."""
        with self._lock:
            if not isinstance(name, str) or not name.strip():
                raise ValidationError("Agent name must be a non-empty string.")
            if not isinstance(max_concurrent_tasks, int) or max_concurrent_tasks < 1:
                raise ValidationError("max_concurrent_tasks must be a positive int.")
            aid = agent_id or f"AGENT-{next(self._agent_seq)}"
            if aid in self._agents:
                raise ValidationError(f"Agent id already exists: {aid!r}")
            agent = Agent(
                id=aid,
                name=name.strip(),
                capabilities=_normalise_resources(capabilities),
                max_concurrent_tasks=max_concurrent_tasks,
            )
            self._agents[aid] = agent
            self._log("AGENT_REGISTERED", agent_id=aid, details=f"name={agent.name}")
            self.schedule()
            return agent

    def remove_agent(self, agent_id: str) -> None:
        """Remove an agent, reassigning any of its active tasks first."""
        with self._lock:
            agent = self._require_agent(agent_id)
            self._release_agent_tasks(agent, reason="agent removed")
            del self._agents[agent_id]
            self._status_board.pop(agent_id, None)
            self._log("AGENT_REMOVED", agent_id=agent_id)
            self.schedule()

    def set_agent_status(self, agent_id: str, status: AgentStatus) -> Agent:
        """Mark an agent available/offline; offline triggers reassignment."""
        with self._lock:
            agent = self._require_agent(agent_id)
            if not isinstance(status, AgentStatus):
                raise ValidationError(f"Invalid agent status: {status!r}")
            previous = agent.status
            agent.status = status
            self._log(
                "AGENT_STATUS_CHANGED",
                agent_id=agent_id,
                details=f"{previous.value} -> {status.value}",
            )
            if status == AgentStatus.OFFLINE and previous != AgentStatus.OFFLINE:
                self._release_agent_tasks(agent, reason="agent became unavailable")
            self.schedule()
            return agent

    def set_agent_available(self, agent_id: str) -> Agent:
        return self.set_agent_status(agent_id, AgentStatus.AVAILABLE)

    def set_agent_offline(self, agent_id: str) -> Agent:
        return self.set_agent_status(agent_id, AgentStatus.OFFLINE)

    def get_agent(self, agent_id: str) -> Agent:
        with self._lock:
            return self._require_agent(agent_id)

    def list_agents(self) -> List[Agent]:
        with self._lock:
            return sorted(self._agents.values(), key=lambda a: a.id)

    def _release_agent_tasks(self, agent: Agent, *, reason: str) -> None:
        """Unassign every active task held by *agent* (does not schedule)."""
        for task_id in list(agent.active_task_ids):
            task = self._tasks.get(task_id)
            if task is not None and task.status.is_active:
                self._unassign(task, reason=reason)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Task definition
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    def add_task(
        self,
        name: str,
        description: str = "",
        *,
        priority: "Priority | int | str" = Priority.MEDIUM,
        required_resources=None,
        estimated_duration: Optional[float] = None,
        task_id: Optional[str] = None,
        auto_schedule: bool = True,
    ) -> Task:
        """Define a new task and enqueue it for scheduling."""
        with self._lock:
            if not isinstance(name, str) or not name.strip():
                raise ValidationError("Task name must be a non-empty string.")
            if estimated_duration is not None:
                if (
                    isinstance(estimated_duration, bool)
                    or not isinstance(estimated_duration, (int, float))
                    or estimated_duration <= 0
                ):
                    raise ValidationError(
                        "estimated_duration must be a positive number of seconds."
                    )
            tid = task_id or f"TASK-{next(self._task_seq)}"
            if tid in self._tasks:
                raise ValidationError(f"Task id already exists: {tid!r}")
            task = Task(
                id=tid,
                name=name.strip(),
                description=description or "",
                priority=Priority.coerce(priority),
                required_resources=_normalise_resources(required_resources),
                estimated_duration=float(estimated_duration)
                if estimated_duration
                else None,
                created_at=self._now(),
                seq=next(self._task_seq),
            )
            self._tasks[tid] = task
            self._log(
                "TASK_CREATED",
                task_id=tid,
                details=f"name={task.name}; priority={task.priority.label}",
            )
            if auto_schedule:
                self.schedule()
            return task

    def get_task(self, task_id: str) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            return self._require_task(task_id)

    def cancel_task(self, task_id: str, reason: str = "") -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            if task.status.is_terminal:
                raise ValidationError(
                    f"Cannot cancel task in terminal state: {task.status.value}"
                )
            self._detach_from_agent(task)
            task.status = TaskStatus.CANCELLED
            task.assigned_agent_id = None
            if reason:
                task.notes.append(f"Cancelled: {reason}")
            self._log("TASK_CANCELLED", task_id=task_id, details=reason)
            self.schedule()
            return task

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Scheduling / assignment
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    def _pending_tasks(self) -> List[Task]:
        tasks = [t for t in self._tasks.values() if t.status == TaskStatus.PENDING]
        # Highest priority first, then FIFO by creation order.
        tasks.sort(key=lambda t: (-int(t.priority), t.seq))
        return tasks

    def _best_agent(self, task: Task) -> Optional[Agent]:
        candidates = [
            a
            for a in self._agents.values()
            if a.is_available() and a.can_handle(task)
        ]
        if not candidates:
            return None
        # Prefer least loaded, then highest success rate, then stable id.
        candidates.sort(key=lambda a: (a.load, -a.success_rate, a.id))
        return candidates[0]

    def _assign(self, task: Task, agent: Agent) -> None:
        task.status = TaskStatus.ASSIGNED
        task.assigned_agent_id = agent.id
        task.assigned_at = self._now()
        agent.active_task_ids.add(task.id)
        self._log(
            "TASK_ASSIGNED",
            task_id=task.id,
            agent_id=agent.id,
            details=f"priority={task.priority.label}",
        )
        self._notify(
            agent.id,
            f"You have been assigned {task.id} '{task.name}' "
            f"({task.priority.label} priority).",
        )

    def _detach_from_agent(self, task: Task) -> Optional[Agent]:
        agent = None
        if task.assigned_agent_id:
            agent = self._agents.get(task.assigned_agent_id)
            if agent is not None:
                agent.active_task_ids.discard(task.id)
        return agent

    def _unassign(self, task: Task, *, reason: str) -> None:
        agent = self._detach_from_agent(task)
        previous_agent = task.assigned_agent_id
        task.assigned_agent_id = None
        task.status = TaskStatus.PENDING
        note = f"Reassigned from {previous_agent or 'unassigned'}: {reason}"
        task.notes.append(note)
        self._log(
            "TASK_REASSIGNED",
            task_id=task.id,
            agent_id=previous_agent,
            details=reason,
        )
        if previous_agent:
            self._notify(
                previous_agent,
                f"Task {task.id} '{task.name}' was unassigned from you ({reason}).",
            )
        # keep the (now stale) agent reference quiet; schedule() re-places task.
        del agent

    def _find_preemptible(self, task: Task) -> Optional[Tuple[Agent, Task]]:
        """Find the lowest-value active task that *task* may preempt."""
        best: Optional[Tuple[Agent, Task]] = None
        best_key: Optional[Tuple[int, float, int]] = None
        for agent in self._agents.values():
            if not agent.is_online or not agent.can_handle(task):
                continue
            for victim_id in agent.active_task_ids:
                victim = self._tasks.get(victim_id)
                if victim is None or victim.priority >= task.priority:
                    continue
                if victim.status not in (TaskStatus.ASSIGNED, TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS):
                    continue
                key = (int(victim.priority), victim.progress, victim.seq)
                if best_key is None or key < best_key:
                    best_key = key
                    best = (agent, victim)
        return best

    def schedule(self) -> int:
        """Assign pending tasks to capable agents. Returns #tasks assigned."""
        with self._lock:
            assigned = 0
            # Pass 1: straightforward assignment by priority.
            for task in self._pending_tasks():
                agent = self._best_agent(task)
                if agent is not None:
                    self._assign(task, agent)
                    assigned += 1

            # Pass 2: urgency-driven preemption for anything still waiting.
            if self.enable_preemption:
                for task in self._pending_tasks():
                    if task.priority < Priority.HIGH:
                        continue
                    found = self._find_preemptible(task)
                    if found is None:
                        continue
                    agent, victim = found
                    self._unassign(
                        victim,
                        reason=(
                            f"preempted by higher-priority {task.id} "
                            f"({task.priority.label})"
                        ),
                    )
                    self._assign(task, agent)
                    self._notify(
                        agent.id,
                        f"Task {victim.id} was preempted to free capacity for "
                        f"the more urgent {task.id}.",
                    )
                    assigned += 1

                # Pass 3: re-place any victims that were bumped above.
                for task in self._pending_tasks():
                    agent = self._best_agent(task)
                    if agent is not None:
                        self._assign(task, agent)
                        assigned += 1
            return assigned

    def assign_task(self, task_id: str, agent_id: str) -> Task:
        """Manually assign a specific task to a specific agent."""
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            agent = self._require_agent(agent_id)
            if task.status.is_terminal:
                raise ValidationError(
                    f"Cannot assign task in terminal state: {task.status.value}"
                )
            if not agent.is_online:
                raise ValidationError(f"Agent {agent_id} is offline.")
            if not agent.can_handle(task):
                missing = sorted(task.required_resources - agent.capabilities)
                raise ValidationError(
                    f"Agent {agent_id} lacks required resources: {missing}"
                )
            if agent.load >= agent.max_concurrent_tasks:
                raise ValidationError(f"Agent {agent_id} is at capacity.")
            if task.status.is_active:
                self._detach_from_agent(task)
            self._assign(task, agent)
            return task

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Execution / progress
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    def start_task(self, task_id: str) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            if task.status not in (TaskStatus.ASSIGNED, TaskStatus.BLOCKED):
                raise ValidationError(
                    f"Task {task_id} must be assigned before it can start "
                    f"(current: {task.status.value})."
                )
            task.status = TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS
            if task.started_at is None:
                task.started_at = self._now()
            self._log("TASK_STARTED", task_id=task_id, agent_id=task.assigned_agent_id)
            return task

    def update_progress(
        self,
        task_id: str,
        progress: Optional[float] = None,
        *,
        note: Optional[str] = None,
        issue: Optional[str] = None,
    ) -> Task:
        """Report real-time progress, notes and/or issues for a task."""
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            if task.status.is_terminal:
                raise ValidationError(
                    f"Cannot update a {task.status.value} task: {task_id}"
                )
            if task.status == TaskStatus.ASSIGNED:
                # First sign of life -> the agent has started working.
                self.start_task(task_id)

            if progress is not None:
                if isinstance(progress, bool) or not isinstance(
                    progress, (int, float)
                ):
                    raise ValidationError("progress must be a number between 0 and 100.")
                task.progress = float(max(0.0, min(100.0, progress)))
            if note:
                task.notes.append(str(note))
            if issue:
                task.issues.append(str(issue))

            self._log(
                "TASK_PROGRESS",
                task_id=task_id,
                agent_id=task.assigned_agent_id,
                details=(
                    f"progress={task.progress:.0f}%"
                    + (f"; note={note}" if note else "")
                    + (f"; issue={issue}" if issue else "")
                ),
            )
            if task.progress >= 100:
                self._complete(task, feedback=None)
            return task

    def report_issue(self, task_id: str, issue: str, *, block: bool = False) -> Task:
        """Record an issue and optionally block the task."""
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            if task.status.is_terminal:
                raise ValidationError(
                    f"Cannot report an issue on a {task.status.value} task."
                )
            if not issue or not str(issue).strip():
                raise ValidationError("Issue description must be non-empty.")
            task.issues.append(str(issue).strip())
            if block:
                task.status = TaskStatus.BLOCKED
            self._log(
                "TASK_ISSUE",
                task_id=task_id,
                agent_id=task.assigned_agent_id,
                details=("blocked: " if block else "") + str(issue).strip(),
            )
            if block and task.assigned_agent_id:
                self._notify(
                    task.assigned_agent_id,
                    f"Task {task_id} is blocked: {issue}",
                )
            return task

    def unblock_task(self, task_id: str) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            if task.status != TaskStatus.BLOCKED:
                raise ValidationError(f"Task {task_id} is not blocked.")
            if task.assigned_agent_id:
                task.status = (
                    TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS
                    if task.started_at is not None
                    else TaskStatus.ASSIGNED
                )
            else:
                task.status = TaskStatus.PENDING
            self._log("TASK_UNBLOCKED", task_id=task_id, agent_id=task.assigned_agent_id)
            self.schedule()
            return task

    def complete_task(self, task_id: str, feedback: Optional[str] = None) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            if task.status.is_terminal:
                raise ValidationError(
                    f"Task {task_id} is already {task.status.value}."
                )
            self._complete(task, feedback=feedback)
            return task

    def _complete(self, task: Task, *, feedback: Optional[str]) -> None:
        now = self._now()
        task.progress = 100.0
        task.completed_at = now
        if task.started_at is None:
            task.started_at = task.assigned_at or now
        task.status = TaskStatus.COMPLETED
        if feedback:
            task.feedback = feedback
            task.notes.append(f"Feedback: {feedback}")
        agent = self._detach_from_agent(task)
        if agent is not None:
            agent.completed_count += 1
            duration = (now - task.started_at).total_seconds()
            agent.total_completion_seconds += max(0.0, duration)
        agent_id = task.assigned_agent_id
        self._log(
            "TASK_COMPLETED",
            task_id=task.id,
            agent_id=agent_id,
            details=feedback or "",
        )
        self._emit(
            agent_id or self.SCHEDULER_ID,
            f"Task {task.id} '{task.name}' completed.",
            type=MessageType.STATUS,
        )
        # Adapt: a freed agent can immediately pull the next queued task.
        self.schedule()

    def fail_task(self, task_id: str, reason: str = "") -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            if task.status.is_terminal:
                raise ValidationError(
                    f"Task {task_id} is already {task.status.value}."
                )
            agent = self._detach_from_agent(task)
            if agent is not None:
                agent.failed_count += 1
            task.status = TaskStatus.FAILED
            task.completed_at = self._now()
            if reason:
                task.issues.append(reason)
            self._log(
                "TASK_FAILED",
                task_id=task_id,
                agent_id=task.assigned_agent_id,
                details=reason,
            )
            self.schedule()
            return task

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Communication
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    def send_message(
        self,
        sender_id: str,
        content: str,
        *,
        to: Optional[str] = None,
    ) -> Message:
        """Send a chat message from one agent to another (or broadcast)."""
        with self._lock:
            if sender_id != self.SCHEDULER_ID:
                self._require_agent(sender_id)
            if to is not None:
                self._require_agent(to)
            if not content or not str(content).strip():
                raise ValidationError("Message content must be non-empty.")
            return self._emit(sender_id, str(content).strip(), recipient=to)

    def broadcast(self, sender_id: str, content: str) -> Message:
        return self.send_message(sender_id, content, to=None)

    def get_messages(
        self,
        agent_id: Optional[str] = None,
        *,
        type: Optional[MessageType] = None,
    ) -> List[Message]:
        """Return messages relevant to *agent_id* (sent, received or broadcast)."""
        with self._lock:
            msgs = list(self._messages)
            if type is not None:
                msgs = [m for m in msgs if m.type == type]
            if agent_id is None:
                return msgs
            return [
                m
                for m in msgs
                if m.recipient is None
                or m.recipient == agent_id
                or m.sender == agent_id
            ]

    def post_status(self, agent_id: str, status_text: str) -> None:
        """Publish a shared status update to the team status board."""
        with self._lock:
            self._require_agent(agent_id)
            if not status_text or not str(status_text).strip():
                raise ValidationError("Status text must be non-empty.")
            self._status_board[agent_id] = (str(status_text).strip(), self._now())
            self._emit(
                agent_id,
                str(status_text).strip(),
                type=MessageType.STATUS,
            )

    def get_status_board(self) -> Dict[str, Tuple[str, datetime]]:
        with self._lock:
            return dict(self._status_board)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Queries: filter / sort / search
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    _SORT_KEYS = {
        "priority": lambda t: (-int(t.priority), t.seq),
        "created": lambda t: t.seq,
        "name": lambda t: t.name.lower(),
        "status": lambda t: t.status.value,
        "progress": lambda t: t.progress,
    }

    def list_tasks(
        self,
        *,
        status: Optional[TaskStatus] = None,
        priority: Optional["Priority | int | str"] = None,
        agent_id: Optional[str] = None,
        resource: Optional[str] = None,
        search: Optional[str] = None,
        sort_by: str = "priority",
        descending: bool = False,
    ) -> List[Task]:
        """Filter, search and sort tasks for the management interface."""
        with self._lock:
            tasks = list(self._tasks.values())
            if status is not None:
                if not isinstance(status, TaskStatus):
                    raise ValidationError(f"Invalid status filter: {status!r}")
                tasks = [t for t in tasks if t.status == status]
            if priority is not None:
                pr = Priority.coerce(priority)
                tasks = [t for t in tasks if t.priority == pr]
            if agent_id is not None:
                tasks = [t for t in tasks if t.assigned_agent_id == agent_id]
            if resource is not None:
                res = str(resource).strip().lower()
                tasks = [t for t in tasks if res in t.required_resources]
            if search:
                tasks = self._search_filter(tasks, search)

            key = self._SORT_KEYS.get(sort_by)
            if key is None:
                raise ValidationError(
                    f"Invalid sort_by {sort_by!r}; choose from "
                    f"{sorted(self._SORT_KEYS)}."
                )
            tasks.sort(key=key, reverse=descending)
            return tasks

    @staticmethod
    def _search_filter(tasks: Sequence[Task], query: str) -> List[Task]:
        q = query.strip().lower()
        out = []
        for t in tasks:
            haystack = " ".join(
                [
                    t.id,
                    t.name,
                    t.description,
                    " ".join(t.required_resources),
                    " ".join(t.notes),
                    " ".join(t.issues),
                ]
            ).lower()
            if q in haystack:
                out.append(t)
        return out

    def search_tasks(self, query: str) -> List[Task]:
        return self.list_tasks(search=query)

    def all_tasks(self) -> List[Task]:
        with self._lock:
            return list(self._tasks.values())

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Monitoring / reporting
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    def agent_performance(self, agent_id: str) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            agent = self._require_agent(agent_id)
            data = agent.to_dict()
            data["active_tasks"] = [
                {"id": t.id, "name": t.name, "progress": round(t.progress, 1)}
                for t in (
                    self._tasks.get(tid) for tid in sorted(agent.active_task_ids)
                )
                if t is not None
            ]
            return data

    def get_history(
        self,
        *,
        task_id: Optional[str] = None,
        agent_id: Optional[str] = None,
        event: Optional[str] = None,
        limit: Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> List[HistoryEntry]:
        with self._lock:
            entries = list(self._history)
            if task_id is not None:
                entries = [e for e in entries if e.task_id == task_id]
            if agent_id is not None:
                entries = [e for e in entries if e.agent_id == agent_id]
            if event is not None:
                entries = [e for e in entries if e.event == event]
            if limit is not None:
                entries = entries[-limit:]
            return entries

    def summary(self) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            counts: Dict[str, int] = {s.value: 0 for s in TaskStatus}
            for t in self._tasks.values():
                counts[t.status.value] += 1
            return {
                "agents": len(self._agents),
                "tasks": len(self._tasks),
                "status_counts": counts,
                "messages": len(self._messages),
                "history_entries": len(self._history),
            }

    def format_dashboard(self) -> str:
        """Render a human readable monitoring dashboard."""
        with self._lock:
            now = self._now()
            lines: List[str] = []
            lines.append("=" * 68)
            lines.append(
                f"  MULTI-AGENT TASK SCHEDULER  |  {now.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')}"
            )
            lines.append("=" * 68)

            # Agents ---------------------------------------------------- #
            lines.append("")
            lines.append("AGENTS")
            lines.append("-" * 68)
            if not self._agents:
                lines.append("  (no agents registered)")
            else:
                lines.append(
                    f"  {'ID':<9}{'NAME':<14}{'STATUS':<9}"
                    f"{'LOAD':<7}{'DONE':<6}{'FAIL':<6}{'SUCCESS':<8}"
                )
                for agent in sorted(self._agents.values(), key=lambda a: a.id):
                    lines.append(
                        f"  {agent.id:<9}{agent.name[:13]:<14}"
                        f"{agent.display_status():<9}"
                        f"{str(agent.load) + '/' + str(agent.max_concurrent_tasks):<7}"
                        f"{agent.completed_count:<6}{agent.failed_count:<6}"
                        f"{agent.success_rate * 100:>5.0f}%  "
                    )

            # Task status summary -------------------------------------- #
            counts = self.summary()["status_counts"]
            lines.append("")
            lines.append("TASKS BY STATUS")
            lines.append("-" * 68)
            lines.append(
                "  "
                + "  ".join(
                    f"{name}={counts[name]}"
                    for name in (
                        "pending",
                        "assigned",
                        "in_progress",
                        "blocked",
                        "completed",
                        "failed",
                        "cancelled",
                    )
                )
            )

            # Pending queue -------------------------------------------- #
            pending = self._pending_tasks()
            lines.append("")
            lines.append("PENDING QUEUE (priority order)")
            lines.append("-" * 68)
            if not pending:
                lines.append("  (queue empty)")
            for t in pending:
                res = ",".join(sorted(t.required_resources)) or "-"
                lines.append(
                    f"  {t.id:<9}{t.priority.label:<9}{t.name[:24]:<25}res:[{res}]"
                )

            # Active work ---------------------------------------------- #
            active = [
                t
                for t in self._tasks.values()
                if t.status in (TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS, TaskStatus.ASSIGNED,
                                TaskStatus.BLOCKED)
            ]
            active.sort(key=lambda t: (-int(t.priority), t.seq))
            lines.append("")
            lines.append("ACTIVE WORK")
            lines.append("-" * 68)
            if not active:
                lines.append("  (nothing in progress)")
            for t in active:
                eta = t.time_to_completion(now)
                lines.append(
                    f"  {t.id:<9}{t.name[:20]:<21}"
                    f"agent:{(t.assigned_agent_id or '-'):<9}"
                    f"{t.progress:>5.0f}%  eta:{format_duration(eta):<8}"
                    f"{'[BLOCKED]' if t.status == TaskStatus.BLOCKED else ''}"
                )

            # Status board --------------------------------------------- #
            if self._status_board:
                lines.append("")
                lines.append("STATUS BOARD")
                lines.append("-" * 68)
                for aid, (text, ts) in sorted(self._status_board.items()):
                    lines.append(f"  {aid:<9}{ts.strftime('%H:%M:%S')}  {text}")

            # Recent activity ------------------------------------------ #
            lines.append("")
            lines.append("RECENT ACTIVITY")
            lines.append("-" * 68)
            for entry in self._history[-6:]:
                lines.append("  " + str(entry))

            lines.append("=" * 68)
            return "\n".join(lines)


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
# Demonstration
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
def demo() -> MultiAgentTaskScheduler:
    """Run a scripted demonstration covering every major feature."""

    # Deterministic, advancing virtual clock so ETA/durations are stable.
    ticker = itertools.count(0)
    base = datetime(2024, 1, 1, 9, 0, 0)

    def clock() -> datetime:
        return base + timedelta(seconds=next(ticker))

    scheduler = MultiAgentTaskScheduler(clock=clock)

    print(">> Registering agents")
    scheduler.register_agent("Ada", ["python", "ml"], max_concurrent_tasks=2)
    scheduler.register_agent("Linus", ["python", "devops"])
    scheduler.register_agent("Grace", ["docs", "qa"])

    print(">> Defining tasks")
    scheduler.add_task(
        "Train model",
        "Train the recommendation model",
        priority="high",
        required_resources=["python", "ml"],
        estimated_duration=120,
    )
    scheduler.add_task(
        "Deploy service",
        "Ship the API to production",
        priority=Priority.MEDIUM,
        required_resources=["devops"],
        estimated_duration=90,
    )
    scheduler.add_task(
        "Write docs",
        "Author user-facing documentation",
        priority="low",
        required_resources=["docs"],
        estimated_duration=60,
    )
    scheduler.add_task(
        "QA regression",
        "Run the regression suite",
        priority="medium",
        required_resources=["qa"],
        estimated_duration=45,
    )

    print(">> Reporting progress and using inter-agent chat")
    scheduler.update_progress("TASK-1", 40, note="features engineered")
    scheduler.post_status("AGENT-1", "Training underway, GPU at 80%")
    scheduler.send_message("AGENT-2", "Need the model artifact when ready.", to="AGENT-1")
    scheduler.broadcast("AGENT-1", "Heads up: model artifact in ~1 min.")

    print(">> A CRITICAL task arrives and preempts lower-priority work")
    scheduler.add_task(
        "Hotfix outage",
        "Mitigate production incident",
        priority="critical",
        required_resources=["devops"],
        estimated_duration=30,
    )

    print(">> An agent goes offline; its work is reassigned automatically")
    scheduler.update_progress("TASK-3", 25, issue="waiting on screenshots")
    scheduler.set_agent_offline("AGENT-3")

    print(">> Completing tasks (freed agents pull queued work)")
    scheduler.complete_task("TASK-1", feedback="AUC improved to 0.92")
    scheduler.set_agent_available("AGENT-3")

    print()
    print(scheduler.format_dashboard())

    print()
    print(">> Search for 'production':")
    for t in scheduler.search_tasks("production"):
        print(f"   - {t.id} {t.name} [{t.status.value}]")

    print()
    print(">> History for TASK-2:")
    for entry in scheduler.get_history(task_id="TASK-2"):
        print("   " + str(entry))

    return scheduler


if __name__ == "__main__":
    demo()
